description: Baseline solver3 raw runtime with existing repo-root autoresearch.sh and enforced solver3 checks.

asi.hypothesis: Establish the current solver3 baseline before touching clique preview, transfer apply, or search-loop sampling.
asi.observation: Clique preview is the single largest hotpath metric; representative/path iteration time still dominates more than any single apply kernel.

description: Guarded progress-only search-loop bookkeeping so callback-free benchmark runs skip acceptance-history and delta-summary updates.

asi.hypothesis: Callback-free solve/benchmark runs spend measurable time maintaining progress-only search stats, so gating them should improve per-iteration runtime without changing results.
asi.rollback_reason: Observed a broad runtime regression, especially in representative/path iteration cost, despite unchanged scores; not safe to keep on a single bad result.
asi.next_action_hint: Target deterministic hotpath costs next: cut clique preview allocation/contains churn or specialize transfer/clique apply paths.
asi.surprise: The code-path-only change did not preserve prior timing shape; likely either hidden interaction or strong environmental variance, so do not build further work on it.

description: Skipped transfer/clique after-group cloning when a source or target slot has no attribute-balance constraints.

asi.hypothesis: Many hotpath cases may have no attribute-balance constraints on touched groups, so skipping after-member cloning in that no-op case should cut preview overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Measured runtime regressed sharply and noisily across unrelated lanes, so this result is not trustworthy enough to keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Re-establish the current noise floor with a no-code-change rerun, then continue with more targeted clique-preview or apply-path changes once measurements stabilize.
asi.observation: The regression pattern hit untouched swap lanes too, which strongly suggests the current local benchmark environment is unstable.

description: No-code-change baseline rerun to measure current local noise floor after two suspicious regressions.

asi.hypothesis: Recent broad regressions may be mostly measurement noise rather than real code effects, so a clean rerun should estimate the current baseline spread.
asi.rollback_reason: This was a measurement-only rerun with no code changes and did not beat the original baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Bias toward improvements larger than ~2µs on solver3_raw_score_us or rerun candidate winners before keeping them.
asi.noise_observation: A clean rerun landed at 35.81µs versus the 33.63µs baseline (~6.5% slower), confirming meaningful local variance across untouched code.

description: Reduced clique-swap search sampling overhead by replacing per-candidate active-member Vec construction with count/source-group checks and removing redundant target-group exclusion scans.

asi.hypothesis: Clique-only search sampling burns time on temporary active-member vectors and redundant target exclusion checks even though source/target groups are already known to differ.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric still regressed, driven by representative iteration time, so it cannot be kept despite better hotpath totals.
asi.next_action_hint: If later runs remain noisy, revisit this exact search-sampling optimization with an immediate confirmation rerun because the micro/hotpath signal was positive.
asi.interesting_signal: hotpath_total_us improved to 13.53µs from 14.61µs and path_avg_iter_us also improved, but representative iteration variance dominated the aggregate.

description: Reworked transfer and clique attribute-balance deltas to count each touched group once and then apply moved-person count adjustments instead of cloning/recounting before-and-after member vectors.

asi.hypothesis: Transfer/clique attribute-balance preview work is wasting time cloning and recounting whole group memberships even though only a few moved people change counts.
asi.what_changed: Replaced before/after member-vector construction with single-pass count extraction plus in-place count deltas for moved people in backend/core/src/solver3/moves/transfer.rs and backend/core/src/solver3/moves/clique_swap.rs.
asi.why_it_helped: The win showed up exactly where expected: transfer/clique preview improved and solve-level iteration also moved in the right direction without any score drift.
asi.follow_up: Confirm the win with one immediate no-code-change rerun, then look for similar count-delta opportunities in other preview paths or constraint checks.

description: Immediate no-code-change confirmation rerun for the attribute-balance count-delta optimization.

asi.hypothesis: If the attribute-balance count-delta optimization is a real win, a no-code-change confirmation rerun should stay near the improved result.
asi.rollback_reason: The confirmation rerun lost overall despite sustained hotpath wins, so the aggregate keep remains low-confidence under current local noise.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the optimization for now because it already beat baseline once, but prioritize changes with larger solve-level gains or confirm future winners with back-to-back reruns before celebrating them.
asi.confirmation_note: Hotpath metrics stayed improved while representative iteration time swung badly again; local representative lanes are currently the least stable part of the aggregate.

description: Extended the count-delta idea to swap attribute-balance previews and used pair-contact totals as a fast path for all-sessions pair-meeting constraints.

asi.hypothesis: The same count-delta technique should help swap attribute-balance previews, and all-sessions pair-meeting counts should be recoverable directly from pair_contacts.
asi.rollback_reason: Path swap iteration cost exploded and aggregate runtime regressed, so this bundle is not safe to keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Back out the swap/pair-meeting bundle and keep mining the already-winning transfer/clique attribute-balance path; inspect swap-specific preview logic separately before touching it again.
asi.failure_shape: Regression concentrated in swap-heavy path metrics, suggesting the swap-side rewrite is the likely culprit rather than the existing transfer/clique keep.

description: Reapplied the earlier clique-search sampling cleanup on top of the kept transfer/clique attribute-balance optimization.

asi.hypothesis: The earlier clique-search sampling cleanup may stack with the kept attribute-balance optimization and turn its hotpath win into a better aggregate result.
asi.rollback_reason: This time the combined change regressed both hotpath_total_us and the aggregate score, so it is not a stable follow-up to keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Stay focused on deterministic preview/apply kernels rather than search-engine sampling tweaks until the representative lanes are more predictable.
asi.lesson: The search-engine clique sampling cleanup is not consistently positive even when transfer/clique preview costs are lower elsewhere.

description: Added a pair-contacts fast path for transfer/clique pair-meeting constraints that cover every session.

asi.hypothesis: When a pair-meeting constraint spans all sessions, the runtime can reuse pair_contacts directly instead of recounting meetings per session.
asi.rollback_reason: Clique preview timing exploded and the aggregate regressed badly, so this micro-optimization is not trustworthy in the current local lane.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not chase pair-meeting shortcuts right now; continue on the proven attribute-balance/counting path or other clearly allocation-heavy preview work.
asi.observation: The failure concentrated in clique-preview metrics despite an apparently tiny code change, reinforcing that the local clique lane is extremely noisy or sensitive.
